message: computeScopeDiff iterates only the pre-task snapshot manifest (manifest.Entries), so files the executor newly creates during the task are never included in the failing-diff artifact. For a task that blocks after only adding new files (a common shape), the diff is empty, writeDiffArtifact returns "", and blocker.ArtifactPath is left empty/absent. This violates the acceptance criterion requiring artifact_path to be present with non-empty content that includes the changed filename(s), and the same omission drops the offending out-of-scope file from out_of_scope artifacts. The repo's tests do not exercise diff content, so the gate stays green.

restoreFromSnapshot short-circuits on a byte-hash match and skips re-applying the snapshot mode, so an in-scope file whose content is unchanged but whose mode changed (e.g. exec bit flipped) is not restored. Mode is only restored on the content-differs branch. This conflicts with the requirements that block-restore reverts executable bits and that idempotent restore reconciles bytes, mode, and symlink target.

loop-summary.json `baseline_green_rate` is computed inversely: buildLoopSummary counts a task as baseline-green only when its baseline Recommendation == "proceed", but per the slice-4a classifier "proceed" means the baseline was RED (no command already green). The baseline-green states are "block" (test-shaped already green) and "signal" (non-test already green). The reported rate therefore measures the fraction of baseline-RED tasks, mislabeled as green, and never counts genuinely-green tasks. This directly violates in-scope item 9's requirement to record real, trustworthy GO/NO-GO signals.

Pre-run invalid_dag handling does not record a structured blocker. The acceptance criterion requires the run to record a per-task structured blocker with reason "invalid_dag" and a why field naming the unresolvable task_id in tasks-state.json. The implementation only writes the error string into Run.Error and leaves every task entry's Blocker nil; no blocker.reason == "invalid_dag" is produced.

The scope.diff failing-diff artifact omits newly-created in-scope files. computeScopeDiff iterates only the start-state snapshot manifest entries, so files the executor created during attempts are never represented. The acceptance criterion requires the diff to cover all files whose content/mode/symlink target changed. If a blocking task's only in-scope changes are new files, computeScopeDiff returns empty, writeDiffArtifact returns "", and blocker.artifact_path is omitted — violating the requirement that artifact_path be present for any block following an executor attempt.

--json output for plan-DAG runs omits the structured blocker details the contract requires. dagRunResponse carries only contract_sha256/terminal_state/passed/task counts and sets a top-level next_commands only for terminal_state "human". The acceptance criteria require --json for blocked/human runs to include the blocked task entry with full blocker details, and for blocked runs there is no next_commands at all. This weakens the non-interactive 'human reached via the driving agent' flow that in-scope item 7 specifies.

Numerous acceptance-criteria-mandated tests are missing; several map onto the deviations above, so the gaps were not caught by the gate. Absent (per the contract's explicit 'A test asserts/covers...' clauses): retry-prompt ordering assertions; snapshot restore of modified/new/deleted/mode/symlink content; dirty-in-scope refusal; out_of_scope detector blocker; paths_out_of_scope lifecycle handling; atomic-write/idempotent-restore of the snapshot manifest; scope.diff artifact assertions; the invalid_dag tasks-state blocker assertions; resume-from-blocked-terminal exit code; the plan-DAG and planless last-result.json gate-consumer-by-reference calls; and the --json structured-blocker assertions. The validation commands (go test/build/make check) cannot detect missing tests.

The DAG executor fixture never creates, modifies, or deletes files, so the slice's core workspace-boundary safety paths are exercised only on a static (no-op) working tree. As a result none of the following are actually tested: out-of-scope escape detection and the out_of_scope blocker (execute_dag.go:591-612, detectOutOfScopeChanges always returns empty so the branch is unreachable); snapshot restore on block including modified/new/deleted/mode/symlink content (execute_dag.go:863-923, restoreFromSnapshot only ever reconciles already-matching files); and the failing-diff scope.diff artifact / artifact_path (execute_dag.go:1034-1076, computeScopeDiff returns "" so writeDiffArtifact yields an empty artifact_path). The contract acceptance criteria explicitly require tests for each of these (out_of_scope blocker with non-empty next_commands; in-scope snapshot restore of modified/new/deleted incl. mode/symlink; scope.diff present, ending /scope.diff, non-empty). These are the slice's central safety guarantees and they ship unverified.

Several other contract-required test behaviors are absent, and the integration tests that exist assert only terminal_state. No test exercises: dirty-in-scope startup refusal (checkDirtyInScope, execute_dag.go:1321-1369); the baseline_green hard-block path (execute_dag.go:513-523 — fixtures use the non-test command 'true', which produces a 'signal' recommendation, never 'block', so the block branch is never hit); retry-feedback prompt content (buildTaskPrompt priorFailure, execute_dag.go:1144-1149 — no test captures the prompt to assert goal-before-context-pack ordering or prior-failure placement); paths_out_of_scope lifecycle; and the entire --json output surface (dagRunResponse). Additionally, TestPlanDAGRequiresHumanStopsRun asserts only terminal_state and omits the required next_commands/--json assertions, and TestPlanDAGBlockedNodeBlocksUpstream never checks blocker.reason or next_commands.

restoreFromSnapshot return errors are discarded at all three call sites (`_ = restoreFromSnapshot(...)`). The scoped restore is the workspace-boundary safety mechanism of this slice; a failed restore is silently hidden and the loop proceeds, after which the next task's buildTaskSnapshot captures the un-restored dirty tree as its baseline, turning a restore failure into silently-accepted state. The error should be surfaced (the contract defines an `error` terminal state for infrastructure-level failures) rather than dropped.

Three persisted-schema struct fields are declared but never populated anywhere in this change: taskStateEntry.SnapshotRef, taskStateEntry.FilesTouched, and loopSummaryDocument.Error. With omitempty they never appear in output, so they are speculative/unused fields added ahead of need. (loopSummaryDocument.Error additionally overlaps a real requirement that loop-summary carry an error description for the `error` terminal state — that completeness angle is for the correctness reviewer; from the over-engineering lens it is an unwired field.)

pactum execute run gained a user-visible plan-DAG execution path (topological loop, new artifacts execute/tasks-state.json + execute/loop-summary.json + execute/tasks/<task_id>/attempts/, terminal states completed|blocked|gate_failed|human|error, structured blockers and --json terminal_state/next_commands), but docs/flow.md was not updated. The Execute artifacts table (line 20) still lists only the single-shot artifacts (execute/dry-run.json, execute/attempts/attempt_NNN/, execute/last-result.json), and the Execute prose section (lines 232-248) describes only single-shot behavior. flow.md is the actively maintained flow reference for this arc (slices 2/3/4a each updated it), and its plan-context paragraph forward-references the now-shipped executor loop, so the omission is an introduced gap.

docs/agents.md is the execution-model doc that flow.md defers to (flow.md:247), but its execute run description (lines 319-330) still presents a single-attempt model ('captures the attempt ... under execute/attempts/') with no mention of the per-task topological loop, per-task attempts under execute/tasks/<task_id>/attempts/, the workspace snapshot/boundary, or the terminal states introduced by this slice. For a plan.tasks[] contract this description is now incomplete and misleading.

restoreFromSnapshot return errors are discarded at all call sites (`_ = restoreFromSnapshot(...)`); a failed restore of the workspace-boundary (the slice's core safety mechanism) silently proceeds and the next task snapshots a contaminated tree. Handle the error: on restore failure stop the run with terminal state error (or human), do not continue.

plan-DAG --json output (dagRunResponse) omits the structured blocker details the contract requires for blocked/human runs (files, why, proposed, next per blocked task). The agent-mediated resolution model depends on these. Add per-task blocker details to the --json output.

computeScopeDiff iterates only the start-state snapshot manifest, so the scope.diff failing-diff artifact omits newly-created in-scope files. Include created files (and populate the FilesTouched state field) so the artifact covers all changed/new/deleted files.

restoreFromSnapshot short-circuits on a byte-hash match and skips re-applying the snapshot mode, so an in-scope file whose content is unchanged but whose mode changed (exec-bit flip) is not restored. Re-apply mode unconditionally on restore.

The plan-DAG execute path is undocumented. Document in docs/flow.md (topological loop, execute/tasks-state.json + loop-summary.json + per-task attempts, terminal states completed|blocked|gate_failed|human|error, structured blockers, --json) and update docs/agents.md's execute run description from the single-attempt model to the per-task loop.

Add missing workspace-boundary tests: dirty-in-scope startup refusal; baseline_green hard-block path with no executor attempt; snapshot restore of modified/new/deleted in-scope files including mode and symlink.
